question: While upgrading a Spring Boot app that uses `camunda.client.mode: saas`, you find the deprecated `camunda.client.cloud.base-url` in the config. Which property is its documented replacement?

a.text: The `camunda.client.cloud` deprecation table lists `camunda.client.cloud.domain` (env `CAMUNDA_CLIENT_CLOUD_DOMAIN`) as the documented replacement for `camunda.client.cloud.base-url`.

b.text: `camunda.client.rest-address` is the unified REST endpoint used when you wire an address directly; it does not compose SaaS connection details from cluster id, region, and domain.

c.text: `camunda.client.cloud.region` selects the geographic region of the SaaS cluster; it is not the base URL.

d.text: There is no `camunda.client.base-url` property in the reference; SaaS connections are assembled from the `cloud.*` properties.

explanation: When `camunda.client.mode: saas`, the client composes its endpoints from `camunda.client.cloud.cluster-id`, `camunda.client.cloud.region`, and `camunda.client.cloud.domain`. The legacy `cloud.base-url` is deprecated in favour of `cloud.domain`.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot Starter - properties reference
